title: Hi (the Valeria tea-shop critique)
html:
TL;DR

  • December 2023 — Yahir's long brutally-honest written response to Valeria's tea-shop business plan (the Té Amo concept being workshopped with Jacoby)
  • Three timeless realities he opens with: extreme sacrifice, never-finance-with-family, breakeven takes years
  • Flags the differentiator gap — "feel like at the moment it's an array of things but not a concentrated effort on a bread and butter"
  • Demolishes the projected unit economics: $24,800/mo revenue is "blatantly naive"; 8400/mo from art classes "you might as well be a thriving full-time gallery"; art-on-walls "$75 piece is still on the wall months later"
  • Strongly advises avoiding Dad as a financing source: "monetary binding with family creates power differentials" + "Dad would absolutely dangle financial debt during episodes"
  • Counter-recommendation: business grants first (especially BIPOC/women-oriented), then small-business loans — frames family financing as the worst option
  • Closes on the founder-mindset doctrine: "if you are not adamantly deadset and determined, don't waste your time, money, and energy"

Notes

This is a remarkable family-counsel artifact: Yahir applying the investment group / crypto club analytical voice to his own sister Valeria's Té Amo tea-and-art-house plan, written one day after Valeria shared it (see 2023 12 29 te amo tea and art house and 2023 12 30 current thoughts on te amo tea and art house for the source plan).

The doc is structured like a paid consultancy memo — Technical (differentiator, addressable market, costs vs revenue), Other (hours of operation, funding bottleneck), and Wrapping Up (verdict: chances slim, wouldn't bet on it, but it's her conviction that matters).

Several cross-summary motifs cluster here:

  • The "9–3 hours" tweet Yahir keeps referencing — small businesses fail because they're closed when their target customer (working millennials) actually has time to visit
  • Family-finance taboo — he explicitly invokes Mom and Dad's restaurant-business pattern + the Dad's episodes dynamic from 2022 10 15 things on 10 1415 2022 as the cautionary tale
  • Two-phase business reality — "first year is survival mode, then maybe smooth sailing" — the same arc he later applies to Rankett/Brotein

The closing posture is the clearest in-vault statement of Yahir-as-honest-counselor: "I will never hate on someone for following a passion or idea... but I would ensure that one doesn't have blinders on." This is the family counsel role in action.

Connects to: te amo, 2023 12 29 te amo tea and art house, 2023 12 30 current thoughts on te amo tea and art house, family as hidden runway.
